response.justification: Mohammed brings genuine depth in high-throughput distributed systems — millions of HTTP requests per second with async I/O and low-latency architecture at Dunnhumby — along with strong TypeScript and current hands-on MCP/agentic tooling work at re:cinq that directly maps to the AI-native engineering environment. However, Python is entirely absent from the resume and skills list despite being a stated must-have, which is a material blocker without further verification. Evidence of cross-functional stakeholder management, technical strategy ownership at scale, and operational on-call responsibility is also thin. A screen call to probe Python proficiency and strategy ownership depth is warranted before committing to a full interview loop.
strengths: Proven high-throughput distributed systems work at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O, caching, and fibers
Current hands-on MCP code generator and agentic workflow tooling at re:cinq directly aligns with the AI-native engineering baseline the role requires
Consistent staff-level titling across multiple employers with broad technical depth spanning systems, web, mobile, and embedded domains
concerns: Python is completely absent from the resume and skills list despite being a stated must-have for the role
Little evidence of cross-functional stakeholder management, product-engineering bridging, or driving technical strategy at organizational scale
AI-native engineering experience is confined to the current role begun April 2025 with no mention of operational on-call ownership or incident-response history
